goal.original: Fix the status-bar progress for LTX (and all workflows): it jumped to 50%/100% and hung instead of showing real per-step feedback. The bar must work BOTH locally and on remote RunPod Pods.
goal.summary: LOCAL path is SHIPPED + live-verified (commit 3a09e4f): a stdout-tqdm-driven per-stage status bar replaces the broken WS-weighted bar. REMOTE (Pod) still falls back to the old WS aggregator because the Pod wrapper does not capture/parse ComfyUI stdout. Remaining work = teach the Pod wrapper (mpi-ci repo) to pipe+parse ComfyUI stdout and broadcast the same SSE events, then live-verify on a Pod.

completed: Local: routes/comfy.js parses ComfyUI stdout tqdm bars -> comfy:step-progress / comfy:tile-progress (USDU) / comfy:segment-total (detailer SEGS) over the /comfy/events/stream SSE
Local: js/services/phaseProgress.js (createStageProgress) — per-bar 0-100% fill + 'Stage N/M' counter, tile() mode for Ultimate, setTotal() for detailer
Local: js/services/commandExecutor.js SSE listeners drive the bar; STEP_EMITTING_KINDS opens the stream for sampler/ultimateSDUpscale/detailer; imageUpscale (ESRGAN, no signal) -> indeterminate pulse
Local: js/data/progressStages.js — recorded bar count per workflow + run mode (single/preview/stage2)
Local: timer/card/toast unified at prompt_ack (tool:accepted) so they match and exclude ComfyUI cold-start boot
Verified live by user: LTX 3/2/1, WAN 2/2, detailer 1/N, Ultimate upscaler (2-tile), universal upscale pulse
pending: Pod wrapper (mpi-ci): capture ComfyUI stdout (add stdout=PIPE in _spawn) + a reader task that parses tqdm lines the SAME way routes/comfy.js does

Pod wrapper: broadcast comfy:step-progress {value,max}, comfy:tile-progress {tile,tiles}, comfy:segment-total {total} via _broadcast_sse() — the existing SSE relay (remoteProxy.js) already pipes these to the app unchanged
Ship the wrapper.py change via R2 no-rebuild (publish-runtime.sh) — no image rebuild needed
Live-verify on a Pod: LTX gen should show the smooth 'Stage N/M' bar identical to local

completed: gotchas.md § 'status-bar progress — WS events are useless, ComfyUI stdout is the truth (MPI-147)' captures the architecture + remote fallback note
Stage-counting procedure documented in comfy_workflows/scripts/workflow_generation/README.md § 'Progress stages' and docs/builder/05-author-and-test.md step 4
pending: After remote ships: update the gotchas remote-fallback sentence to 'remote now parses stdout too'

User raised: consider a more prominent doc callout that a NEW step-emitting node type also needs its class_type added to STEP_EMITTING_KINDS (commandExecutor.js) + buildWeightMap kinds (progressAggregator.js) — currently mentioned but not a checklist line

next_action.description: In the mpi-ci repo at c:\AI\Mpi\mpi-ci\cubric-vision-pod\wrapper\wrapper.py: (1) in ComfyManager._spawn() (~line 248) add stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE (and merge stderr) to create_subprocess_exec; (2) add an async reader task draining proc.stdout line-by-line that parses tqdm (port the regexes from routes/comfy.js _handleComfyOutput: tqdm /(\d+)\/(\d+)\s*\[/g taking the LAST match, skip max<=1 model-load bars for step-progress but route USDU: lines to tile-progress and '# of Detected SEGS: N' to segment-total) and calls _broadcast_sse(...) with the SAME event shapes the app expects. Then publish via publish-runtime.sh (R2 no-rebuild) and live-verify an LTX gen on a Pod shows the smooth Stage N/M bar. Close MPI-147 when verified.

key_decisions: WS progress_state is useless for video/upscale (ComfyUI 0.26 reports slow phases — model-init, VAE — as binary 0/1; LTX samplers are tiny). ComfyUI STDOUT (tqdm) is the only real signal. This is the whole basis of the fix.
Bar runs 0-100% PER tqdm bar; status bar shows 'Stage N/M' so each reset reads as a new stage, not a bug. (User's idea, replaced an earlier weighted-aggregation attempt that kept failing.)

Stage count M is RECORDED by hand per workflow + run mode in js/data/progressStages.js — it cannot be derived from the JSON (model-load bar isn't a node; LTX schedulers compute step counts at runtime; same file differs single vs preview vs stage2).

UltimateSDUpscale self-declares tiles via 'USDU: t/T'; detailer self-declares segments via '# of Detected SEGS: N' — no progressStages entry needed for those. ImageUpscaleWithModel (ESRGAN) emits NO signal -> indeterminate pulse.
timer/card/toast anchor at prompt_ack (tool:accepted) — earlier attempts anchored at dispatch (counted ComfyUI cold-start boot ~15s) or at sampling-start (card < toast mismatch).
constraints: The Pod wrapper currently does NOT capture ComfyUI stdout: create_subprocess_exec(*cmd) with no stdout=PIPE (wrapper.py ~line 248). It only SYNTHESIZES model-init events from WS (ModelInitSynth ~line 385). No tqdm parsing exists on the Pod.

The SSE relay already works: remoteProxy.js /comfy/events/stream pipes the Pod's /wrapper/events/stream straight through. So whatever the wrapper _broadcast_sse()s reaches the app's commandExecutor SSE listeners unchanged. Only the PRODUCER (wrapper stdout parse) is missing.

The app's remote path ALREADY falls back gracefully: with no comfy:step-progress events, _stdoutDriving stays false and the WS aggregator drives the bar (old behavior, not frozen, just not smooth). So shipping the wrapper change is purely additive — no app change needed for remote.
Ship wrapper.py via R2 no-rebuild (publish-runtime.sh) — do NOT rebuild the image for a wrapper-only change. See cubric-vision-pod/bootstrap.sh + publish-runtime.sh.

Event shapes MUST match the app exactly: comfy:step-progress {value,max}, comfy:tile-progress {tile,tiles}, comfy:segment-total {total}. See the addEventListener blocks in js/services/commandExecutor.js (~lines 1085-1145).

Shared git tree — commit by explicit pathspec, never git add . ; the mpi-ci repo is separate (use git -C c:/AI/Mpi/mpi-ci). The MPI-156 keepalive change in routes/remoteProxy.js is NOT part of MPI-147 — leave it.
User noted MPI-156 (Pod image builds) is in flight — this remote work pairs naturally with that session.
files_to_read_first: routes/comfy.js (the _handleComfyOutput tqdm/USDU/SEGS parse to PORT to the Pod)
js/services/commandExecutor.js (SSE listener event shapes the wrapper must match)

rules_active: docs/gotchas.md (§ status-bar progress, § RunPod / remote engine)
resume_prompt: Read .agents/mpi-kanban/state/handoffs/98bce010-2d44-49dd-95af-dd09d366ff95.json and use mpi-continue to resume MPI-147. Context: the LTX/status-bar progress fix is SHIPPED + verified for LOCAL generations (commit 3a09e4f) — it drives the bar from ComfyUI stdout tqdm parsing (routes/comfy.js -> SSE -> phaseProgress.js -> statusBar), showing 'Stage N/M' per workflow. The REMAINING work is REMOTE (RunPod Pod): the Pod wrapper at c:\AI\Mpi\mpi-ci\cubric-vision-pod\wrapper\wrapper.py does NOT capture ComfyUI stdout, so remote gens fall back to the old (smooth-less) WS bar. Your task: in wrapper.py, add stdout=PIPE to the ComfyUI subprocess in _spawn() and add an async reader that parses tqdm lines the SAME way routes/comfy.js _handleComfyOutput does, broadcasting comfy:step-progress {value,max}, comfy:tile-progress {tile,tiles}, and comfy:segment-total {total} via _broadcast_sse(). The SSE relay already pipes these to the app unchanged — no app change needed. Ship via publish-runtime.sh (R2 no-rebuild), then live-verify an LTX gen on a Pod shows the smooth Stage N/M bar, and close MPI-147. Read routes/comfy.js and js/services/commandExecutor.js first for the exact regexes and event shapes. mpi-ci is a separate git repo (git -C). Do not touch the MPI-156 keepalive in remoteProxy.js.
